journal_red_flags: Omitting computational complexity and efficiency analysis (e.g., FLOPs, latency, FPS, memory usage, hardware resource consumption)
Missing standard video-specific objective quality metrics (e.g., PSNR, SSIM, BD-rate, bitrate, mAP) in comparative studies
Purely theoretical or mathematical formulations without practical video, circuit, system, or hardware implementation considerations
Absence of ablation studies to justify the necessity of individual proposed architectural components or modules
Relying solely on image classification or non-video datasets to validate a general-purpose vision/video backbone without extensive validation on standard video datasets
Using overly dense or complex mathematical proofs for standard deep learning operations or simple architectural modifications
Failing to compare against recent state-of-the-art baselines (e.g., Transformers, GNNs) or relying solely on qualitative results without rigorous quantitative benchmarking
Overly long philosophical or historical introductions without a quick transition to the specific technical motivation and application scenario
